title: nauclea hirsuta
intro: Nauclea hirsuta, also known as Quinine Tree or Kudaidae, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The medicinal properties of Nauclea hirsuta lie in its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to the presence of compounds such as naucleatin and naucleagenin. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antimalarial agent. Nauclea hirsuta can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional herbal medicine.
